title: maesa haenkeana
intro: Maesa haenkeana, also known as the 'Kakau' or 'Borong', is a plant species in the family Primulaceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a treatment for various gastrointestinal issues. In terms of herbal preparations, Maesa haenkeana is often used as a decoction, made by steeping the leaves and roots in water, to treat various ailments.
